message:
fix(autocomplete): use platform path separator for directory completions on Windows

Path completion was hardcoded to append '/' for directory entries, which
broke Windows backslash-style input (e.g. typing "src\n" would produce
"src\nested/" instead of "src\nested\"). The fix uses the platform
separator variable that was already computed earlier in the function but
not passed through to the directory-suffix logic.

Adds a test (path_completion_windows_directory_input_preserves_backslashes_for_directories)
that verifies backslash-based directory input preserves backslashes in
the completion output and never mixes separators.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
